description: Technical knowledge base K: procedures and critical parameters for PT (Penlight) gear-box assembly tasks (HA-ViD case study).

procedure: 1. Pick up the shaft.
2. Locate Gear Hole 1 on the gear plate.
3. Align the longer threaded end of the shaft with Gear Hole 1.
4. Begin threading by hand — turn clockwise with light downward pressure.
5. Continue turning by hand until the shaft is finger-tight (resistance clearly increases).
6. Do not over-tighten by hand; the wrench step follows.

critical_parameters.note: Counter-clockwise will strip the thread
Final torque applied in sftg1ws using Wrench-Shaft
Do not confuse with Gear Hole 2 — holes are adjacent

common_errors: Screwing the shorter threaded end of the shaft into Gear Hole 1
Cross-threading due to misalignment before turning
Over-tightening by hand — can damage thread before wrench step

sftg1ws.task_name: Screw Shaft into Gear Hole 1 using Wrench-Shaft
procedure: 1. Retrieve the Wrench-Shaft.
2. Fit the wrench onto the shaft head at Gear Hole 1.
3. Apply controlled clockwise torque until the shaft is firmly seated.
4. Stop when resistance sharply increases — do not force further.
5. Verify the shaft does not wobble; it should be rigid in the gear plate.
6. Return the Wrench-Shaft to the tool tray.

critical_parameters.note: Do not substitute with pliers — will damage shaft head
Wobble indicates insufficient torque
Over-torquing will strip the housing thread

common_errors: Using wrong tool (Wrench-Nut instead of Wrench-Shaft)
Under-torquing — shaft wobbles and will loosen during operation
Over-torquing — strips gear housing thread

procedure: 1. Pick up the shaft.
2. Locate Gear Hole 2 on the gear plate.
3. Align the longer threaded end of the shaft with Gear Hole 2.
4. Begin threading by hand — turn clockwise with light downward pressure.
5. Continue turning by hand until finger-tight.
6. Do not over-tighten by hand; the wrench step follows.

critical_parameters.note: Counter-clockwise will strip the thread
Final torque applied in sftg2ws using Wrench-Shaft
Do not confuse with Gear Hole 1

common_errors: Screwing the shorter threaded end of the shaft into Gear Hole 2
Cross-threading due to initial misalignment

sftg2ws.task_name: Screw Shaft into Gear Hole 2 using Wrench-Shaft
procedure: 1. Retrieve the Wrench-Shaft.
2. Fit the wrench onto the shaft head at Gear Hole 2.
3. Apply controlled clockwise torque until firmly seated.
4. Stop when resistance sharply increases.
5. Verify no wobble in the shaft.
6. Return the Wrench-Shaft to the tool tray.

critical_parameters.note: Same tool as sftg1ws
Match tightness of Shaft 1 for symmetric load
Symmetric torque on both shafts is important

common_errors: Using wrong tool (Wrench-Nut instead of Wrench-Shaft)
Under-torquing — shaft wobbles and will loosen during operation

sspg3.task_name: Screw Phillips Screw into Gear Hole 3
procedure: 1. Pick up the Phillips screw.
2. Locate Gear Hole 3 on the gear plate.
3. Align the screw tip with the hole across the hole on the Worm Gear.
4. Begin threading clockwise by hand — apply gentle downward pressure.
5. Finger-tighten until resistance increases.
6. Screwdriver tightening follows in sspg3dp.

critical_parameters.note: Ensure correct screw is used — not flat-head
Critical for worm gear alignment
Over-tightening by hand damages the Phillips head

common_errors: Wrong screw type (flat-head instead of Phillips)
Screwing without the Worm Gear
Cross-threading — always align before turning

sspg3dp.task_name: Screw Phillips Screw into Gear Hole 3 using Phillips Screwdriver
procedure: 1. Retrieve the Phillips Screwdriver.
2. Seat the screwdriver bit firmly into the screw head — ensure full engagement.
3. Apply clockwise torque with steady downward pressure.
4. Stop when the screw is fully seated and resistance sharply increases.
5. Do not cam-out (slip) — this damages the screw head.
6. Return the Phillips Screwdriver.

critical_parameters.note: Ensure correct bit size — PH1 or PH2 as specified
Partial engagement causes cam-out and head damage
Screw head should be flush with or slightly below housing surface

common_errors: Partial bit engagement causing cam-out and stripped screw head
Under-tightening — screw will loosen under vibration
Wrong screwdriver size

pgwg3.task_name: Place Worm Gear onto Gear Hole 3
procedure: 1. Pick up the Worm Gear component.
2. Orient the worm gear so its hole aligns with the Gear Hole 3 on the gear plate.
3. Lower the worm gear onto the plate.
4. Ensure the worm gear meshes with the adjacent gear teeth, if the adjacent gear teeth are not present, skip this step.

critical_parameters.value: aling the hole on the worm gear with the Gear Hole 3 on the gear plate
critical_parameters.note: Incorrect orientation prevents gear meshing
Verify mesh before proceeding — misalignment causes binding
Resistance indicates misalignment or wrong part

common_errors: Incorrect orientation
Gear not meshing — placed on plate but not engaged with adjacent gear

procedure: 1. Pick up the Large Placer.
2. Push the placer down onto Shaft 1.
3. Confirm the placer seats firmly on the Shaft 1.

critical_parameters.note: Large Placer has larger inner diameter — verify before placing
Do not confuse with Shaft 2
Partial seating misaligns the Large Gear

common_errors: Using Small Placer instead of Large Placer — will not seat correctly
Placing on Shaft 2 instead of Shaft 1
Partial seating — placer tilted on shaft

procedure: 1. Pick up the Large Placer.
2. Push the placer down onto Shaft 2.
3. Confirm the placer seats firmly on the Shaft 2.

critical_parameters.note: Same part as used on Shaft 1
Symmetric counterpart to iplf1
Partial seating misaligns the Small Gear

common_errors: Using Small Placer instead of Large Placer — will not seat correctly
Placing on Shaft 1 instead of Shaft 2
Partial seating — placer tilted on shaft

procedure: 1. Pick up the Large Gear.
2. Align the gear bore with Shaft 1 (flat plate facing downward).
3. Lower the gear onto the shaft util it seats firmly on the Large Placer.
4. Ensure the gear teeth mesh correctly with the adjacent gears, if the adjacent gears are not present, skip this step.

critical_parameters.note: Gears are visually similar — verify by comparing diameter

critical_parameters.value: Shaft 1, on top of Large Placer
critical_parameters.note: Large Placer must be seated before this step
flat plate seats on the Large Placer

critical_parameters.value: teeth engaged with the adjacent gears
critical_parameters.note: Verify mesh — binding indicates misalignment

common_errors: Using Small Gear instead of Large Gear
Inserting before Large Placer is seated
Facing the flat plate upward instead of downward
Gear not meshing with the adjacent gears

procedure: 1. Pick up the Small Gear.
2. Align the gear bore with Shaft 2 (flat plate facing downward).
3. Lower the gear onto the shaft util it seats firmly on the Large Placer.
4. Ensure the gear teeth mesh correctly with the adjacent gears, if the adjacent gears are not present, skip this step.

critical_parameters.note: Gears are visually similar — verify by comparing diameter

critical_parameters.value: Shaft 2, on top of Large Placer
critical_parameters.note: Large Placer must be seated before this step
flat plate seats on the Large Placer

critical_parameters.value: teeth engaged with the adjacent gears
critical_parameters.note: Verify mesh — binding indicates misalignment

common_errors: Using Large Gear instead of Small Gear
Inserting before Large Placer is seated
Facing the flat plate upward instead of downward
Gear not meshing with the adjacent gears

procedure: 1. Pick up the Small Placer.
2. Place the Small Placer onto Shaft 1 on top of the Large Gear.
3. Push down until it seats against the top of the Large Gear.
4. Confirm the Small Placer is flat and fully seated.

critical_parameters.note: Goes above Large Gear — do not confuse with Large Placer

critical_parameters.value: on top of Large Gear, Shaft 1
critical_parameters.note: Order: Gear Plate → Large Placer → Large Gear → Small Placer → Nut
Tilted placer will cause nut to seat incorrectly

common_errors: Using Large Placer instead of Small Placer
Incorrect assembly order — Small Placer placed before Large Gear
Placer seated at angle

procedure: 1. Pick up the Small Placer.
2. Place the Small Placer onto Shaft 2 on top of the Small Gear.
3. Push down until it seats against the top of the Small Gear.
4. Confirm the Small Placer is flat and fully seated.

critical_parameters.note: Goes above Small Gear — do not confuse with Large Placer

critical_parameters.value: on top of Small Gear, Shaft 2
critical_parameters.note: Order: Gear Plate → Large Placer → Large Gear → Small Placer → Nut
Tilted placer will cause nut to seat incorrectly

common_errors: Using Large Placer instead of Small Placer
Incorrect assembly order — Small Placer placed before Small Gear
Placer seated at angle

procedure: 1. Pick up a Nut.
2. Place the Nut onto the shorter threaded end of Shaft 1 (above Small Placer).
3. Begin threading clockwise by hand.
4. Finger-tighten until the Nut contacts the Small Placer.
5. Wrench tightening follows.

critical_parameters.note: Prevents cross-threading
Final torque applied in sntf1wn
Ensure Nut is on correct the Shaft

common_errors: Cross-threading — always hand-start before using wrench
Over-tightening by hand
Wrong shaft

sntf1wn.task_name: Screw Nut onto Shaft 1 using Wrench-Nut
procedure: 1. Retrieve the Wrench-Nut.
2. Fit the wrench onto the Nut on Shaft 1.
3. Tighten clockwise until firmly seated — sharp resistance indicates full tightening.
4. Verify the assembly stack (placer + gear + placer + nut) is secure.
5. Return the Wrench-Nut.

critical_parameters.note: Do not use Wrench-Shaft — different jaw size
Over-torquing can crack the gear plate
Gear should be retained with no axial movement

common_errors: Using Wrench-Shaft instead of Wrench-Nut
Over-torquing — risk of damage
Nut not fully seated on placer before using wrench

procedure: 1. Pick up a Nut.
2. Place the Nut onto the shorter threaded end of Shaft 2 (above Small Placer).
3. Begin threading clockwise by hand.
4. Finger-tighten until the Nut contacts the Small Placer.
5. Wrench tightening follows.

critical_parameters.note: Prevents cross-threading
Final torque applied in sntf1wn
Ensure Nut is on correct the Shaft

common_errors: Cross-threading — always hand-start before using wrench
Over-tightening by hand
Wrong shaft

sntf2wn.task_name: Screw Nut onto Shaft 2 using Wrench-Nut
procedure: 1. Retrieve the Wrench-Nut.
2. Fit the wrench onto the Nut on Shaft 2.
3. Tighten clockwise until firmly seated — sharp resistance indicates full tightening.
4. Verify the assembly stack (placer + gear + placer + nut) is secure.
5. Return the Wrench-Nut.

critical_parameters.note: Do not use Wrench-Shaft — different jaw size
Over-torquing can crack the gear plate
Gear should be retained with no axial movement

common_errors: Using Wrench-Shaft instead of Wrench-Nut
Over-torquing — risk of damage
Nut not fully seated on placer before using wrench

procedure: 1. Confirm all three parallel assembly chains are complete (sntf1wn, sntf2wn, sspg3dp all done).
2. Grasp the Worm Gear lightly between thumb and forefinger.
3. Rotate the Worm Gear a full 360° in both directions.
4. Verify the gear train (Large Gear on Shaft 1) engages and rotates smoothly.
5. Any binding or resistance indicates a misalignment in previous steps.
6. If smooth rotation is confirmed, the sub-assembly is complete.

critical_parameters.note: sntf1wn + sntf2wn + sspg3dp must all be done first
One-direction rotation may hide binding
Any roughness requires disassembly to identify root cause

common_errors: Performing Rotate Worm Gear before all prerequisite steps are complete
Only rotating one direction — misses binding in reverse
Accepting slight binding as normal — investigate and correct
